description: The boat: the built thing that floats and carries. 'lupho' (float) plus 'nu', beside 'luphore' (river), which grew from the same verb — the float family holds the moving water and what rides it. Takes 'rewa' (oar) as its natural companion.
sound_symbolism: The whole verb 'lupho' is the hull, and 'nu' closes it small and buoyant. Say it and the stresses bob.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'luphonu mua moriwhe lupho.' (The boat floats on the sea.) 'luphonu mia kolua.' (The boat carries me.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Wind and current still move freight for free. The sail's return is one of the futures the past already built.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rivers were the roads that carried what roads could not. Most old towns stand where a boat could be loaded.
